title: nvm_write: deleted binding is always-true dead code
description: In nvm_write, after calling self.nvm_delete(id)?, the code does: let deleted = true; The variable is then used in map_add_err only in the branch 'if deleted { ... }'. Because the delete call is followed by ? (early-return on error), deleted is always true at that point. The if deleted branch can never be false. The binding adds noise; a future reader might think deleted could be false and waste time tracing it. Suggested rewrite: remove let deleted = true entirely; simplify map_add_err to always return WolfHsmError::DataLost { id: id.0 }.
